target: c_k(q,r) <= C*r^d on r in [1,10], uniform in k, d=log(lambda)/log3, availability-grade (<=12 at r=4.44)
verdict: STUCK on availability-grade; salvageable: exponent d rigorous, no-blow-up reduces to arc-incidence lemma
d_derivation: lambda=N_{k+1}/N_k level-stable 3.372/3.348/3.362 (L6/L7/L8); extent-ratio EXACTLY 3 = det(M)^{1/3} (M elliptic, det 27, all eigenvalue moduli 3), measured 3.0005/3.0002/3.0000; d=log(lambda)/log3=1.104

induction_step_measured.M_maps_r_cover_to_3r_cover_monotone: M.W_k SUBSET W_{k+1} as side-{3,6,9} cells: VERIFIED True at L6,L7,L8 (covering monotonicity holds exactly)

kappa_M_box_distortion.note: GROWS with r (not O(1)) because ||M||_inf=4 > 3=det^{1/3}: isotropic cube-cover leaks the anisotropy factor => induction does NOT carry the lambda multiplier with a uniform constant

per_3x_scale_box_ratio_N_b_over_N_3b.note: reaches lambda=3.36 only at b>=27; the availability window r in [1,10] is PRE-ASYMPTOTIC (ratio 1.12->3.0), so C*r^{1.10} does not govern it

availability.model_independent_fact: measured load-bearing c(4.44)=12 already EQUALS threshold with zero margin; NO functional bound (power-law or affine) can be both valid and <12 at r=4.44

residual_lemma: SAME anti-stacking / arc-incidence lemma as all prior routes: a Cheb-r ball meets O(r) connector-arcs, not the cubic packing count. Route 3 derives the exponent and confirms covering monotonicity + O(1) conversion, but does NOT discharge the constant; and even granting the lemma in power-law form the constant overshoots, so availability needs the TIGHT distribution-invariance (c(4) stays <=12), a strictly stronger statement.
